---
title: Lesson Path
description: Recommended path through the FreeCampus Python course.
execute:
  echo: true
categories: [course]
order: 10
---

## How to use this course

If you are new to programming, follow the lessons in order. If you already know
some Python, use the quizzes, debugging corners, and challenges to decide where
to start.

::: {.callout-tip title="Key idea"}
Programming is not memorizing every command. Programming is learning how to
break a problem into small steps, run experiments, read feedback, and improve.
:::

## Course map

::: {.course-grid}

::: {.course-card}
### 1. [Getting Started](getting-started/index.qmd)

Set up your learning environment and understand how Python runs before memorizing syntax.

- [What Is Programming?](getting-started/what-is-programming.qmd)
- [Google Colab](getting-started/google-colab.qmd)
- [Local Python Tools](getting-started/local-python-tools.qmd)
:::

::: {.course-card}
### 2. [Core Python](core-python/index.qmd)

Learn the small building blocks that almost every Python program uses: values, names, decisions, and repetition.

- [Values, Variables, and Types](core-python/values-variables-types.qmd)
- [Strings, Input, and Output](core-python/strings-input-output.qmd)
- [Booleans and Conditionals](core-python/booleans-and-conditionals.qmd)
- [Loops and Tracing](core-python/loops-and-tracing.qmd)
:::

::: {.course-card}
### 3. [Data Structures](data-structures/index.qmd)

Store, organize, search, and transform many values with the right container for the job.

- [Lists](data-structures/lists.qmd)
- [Tuples](data-structures/tuples.qmd)
- [Dictionaries](data-structures/dictionaries.qmd)
- [Sets](data-structures/sets.qmd)
- [Nested Data](data-structures/nested-data.qmd)
:::

::: {.course-card}
### 4. [Functions](functions/index.qmd)

Package useful steps behind names so code can be reused, tested, and explained.

- [Function Basics](functions/function-basics.qmd)
- [Function Contracts](functions/function-contracts.qmd)
- [Decomposition and Testing](functions/decomposition-and-testing.qmd)
:::

::: {.course-card}
### 5. [Debugging](debugging/index.qmd)

Read errors calmly, inspect state, and make small experiments instead of guessing.

- [Error Messages](debugging/error-messages.qmd)
- [Debugging Tools](debugging/debugging-tools.qmd)
- [Minimal Reproducible Examples](debugging/minimal-reproducible-examples.qmd)
:::

::: {.course-card}
### 6. [Files and Data](files-and-data/index.qmd)

Read, write, validate, and recover from problems when programs interact with data outside memory.

- [Paths and Text Files](files-and-data/paths-and-text-files.qmd)
- [Structured Files](files-and-data/structured-files.qmd)
- [Exceptions and Validation](files-and-data/exceptions-and-validation.qmd)
:::

::: {.course-card}
### 7. [Projects and Environments](projects-and-environments/index.qmd)

Organize code into files, install dependencies safely, and run programs as small projects.

- [Modules and Packages](projects-and-environments/modules-and-packages.qmd)
- [Environments and Dependencies](projects-and-environments/environments-and-dependencies.qmd)
- [Command-Line Programs](projects-and-environments/command-line-programs.qmd)
- [Project Layout](projects-and-environments/project-layout.qmd)
:::

::: {.course-card}
### 8. [Code Quality](code-quality/index.qmd)

Make code trustworthy with tests, style, formatting, linting, types, and automation.

- [Testing with pytest](code-quality/testing-with-pytest.qmd)
- [Style, Formatting, and Linting](code-quality/style-formatting-and-linting.qmd)
- [Type Checking](code-quality/type-checking.qmd)
- [Pre-commit and CI](code-quality/pre-commit-and-ci.qmd)
:::

::: {.course-card}
### 9. [Object-Oriented Python](object-oriented-python/index.qmd)

Use classes when data and behavior belong together, while avoiding unnecessary complexity.

- [Object-Oriented Basics](object-oriented-python/oop-basics.qmd)
- [Dataclasses](object-oriented-python/dataclasses.qmd)
- [Object-Oriented Design](object-oriented-python/oop-design.qmd)
:::

::: {.course-card}
### 10. [Advanced Python Patterns](advanced-python-patterns/index.qmd)

Learn powerful Python patterns one at a time after core concepts are stable.

- [Comprehensions](advanced-python-patterns/comprehensions.qmd)
- [Iterables and Generators](advanced-python-patterns/iterables-and-generators.qmd)
- [Decorators](advanced-python-patterns/decorators.qmd)
- [Context Managers](advanced-python-patterns/context-managers.qmd)
- [Logging and Configuration](advanced-python-patterns/logging-and-configuration.qmd)
:::

::: {.course-card}
### 11. [Scientific Python](scientific-python/index.qmd)

Use Python's scientific stack for arrays, simulation, plotting, symbolic math, and numerical methods.

- [NumPy](scientific-python/numpy.qmd)
- [Random Simulation](scientific-python/random-simulation.qmd)
- [Matplotlib Basics](scientific-python/matplotlib-basics.qmd)
- [SymPy Symbolic Math](scientific-python/sympy-symbolic-math.qmd)
- [SciPy Overview](scientific-python/scipy-overview.qmd)
:::

::: {.course-card}
### 12. [Data Science](data-science/index.qmd)

Turn questions and datasets into cleaned, analyzed, visualized, and communicated results.

- [Workflow and Loading Data](data-science/workflow-and-loading.qmd)
- [Inspecting, Selecting, and Cleaning](data-science/inspecting-selecting-cleaning.qmd)
- [Grouping and Statistics](data-science/grouping-and-statistics.qmd)
- [Visualization with seaborn](data-science/visualization-with-seaborn.qmd)
- [Communicating Results](data-science/communicating-results.qmd)
:::

::: {.course-card}
### 13. [Machine Learning and AI](machine-learning-ai/index.qmd)

Understand AI vocabulary, train simple models, evaluate them responsibly, and know where major libraries fit.

- [Concepts and Datasets](machine-learning-ai/concepts-and-datasets.qmd)
- [Training and Evaluation](machine-learning-ai/training-and-evaluation.qmd)
- [Deep Learning Libraries](machine-learning-ai/deep-learning-libraries.qmd)
- [Transformers Overview](machine-learning-ai/transformers-overview.qmd)
- [Responsible AI](machine-learning-ai/responsible-ai.qmd)
:::

::: {.course-card}
### 14. [Capstone](capstone/index.qmd)

Plan, build, test, document, and present a beginner-friendly Python project that integrates the course.

- [Project Selection and Planning](capstone/project-selection-and-planning.qmd)
- [Testing and README](capstone/testing-and-readme.qmd)
- [Presentation and Rubric](capstone/presentation-and-rubric.qmd)
:::


:::

## Lesson pattern

Lessons are intentionally **cohesive**, not microscopic. A topic like lists can
include indexing, slicing, methods, and copying on one page because those ideas
belong to one container. When a page has multiple sections, it can also include
multiple OJS quizzes so students can check understanding before continuing.

## Study routine for every lesson

1. Read the analogy first.
2. Predict what each short code example will do.
3. Run it in Colab, Jupyter, or VS Code.
4. Answer the section quiz.
5. Review if needed before continuing.
6. Change one small thing and run again.
7. Explain the result in your own words.

## Course checkpoint

::: {.quiz-box}
This OJS quiz checks whether you understand how to study the course.
:::

<script type="application/json" class="fcpython-ojs-quiz-config">
{
  "id": "lesson-path-overview",
  "title": "Lesson path overview checkpoint",
  "instructions": "Choose an answer, then check your work.",
  "questions": [
    {
      "id": "lesson-size",
      "prompt": "What is the current lesson organization principle?",
      "options": [
        "One page per coherent learner topic, with multiple quizzes when useful.",
        "One page for every tiny sub-skill.",
        "One page for the entire course.",
        "No quizzes until the capstone."
      ],
      "answer_index": 0,
      "explanation": "Related sub-skills should live together when they form one concept, such as lists."
    },
    {
      "id": "study-habit",
      "prompt": "What should you do after each section quiz?",
      "options": [
        "Review if uncertain, then move to the next section.",
        "Skip all examples.",
        "Delete your notes.",
        "Change many lines at once."
      ],
      "answer_index": 0,
      "explanation": "Short review loops make the next section easier."
    }
  ]
}
</script>

{{< include _includes/ojs-quiz.qmd >}}
